definition: WhatsApp Business API users can display product catalogs directly inside chat conversations, allowing customers to browse, search, and add items to a cart without leaving WhatsApp. WooCommerce and Shopify store owners can sync their existing product catalogs to WhatsApp through Business Solution Providers (BSPs), creating an in-chat shopping experience that mirrors their online store's product structure.
target_audience: WordPress/WooCommerce store owners who want to sell through WhatsApp. Also relevant for Shopify merchants and any ecommerce business using the WhatsApp Business API. Requires API access (not available on the free WhatsApp Business App beyond basic catalog display).
key_features: In-chat product catalogs: Customers can browse your full product catalog within WhatsApp, organized by the same categories as your WooCommerce store.
Catalog message templates: Send product recommendations or specific items as rich catalog messages through template messages.
Quick reply product cards: Customers can tap to view product details, see images and pricing, and add items to an in-chat cart.

WooCommerce sync via BSPs: Platforms like Interakt, Hello24, Wati, Spur, and Tellephant offer WordPress/WooCommerce plugins that sync your product catalog, orders, and customer data to WhatsApp automatically.
Multi-product messages: Send a curated selection of up to 30 products from your catalog in a single message.
Single-product messages: Highlight one specific product with image, description, and price for targeted recommendations.

Order placement within chat: Customers can complete their purchase flow inside WhatsApp (cart building and checkout), though payment processing typically redirects to your WooCommerce checkout or uses a BSP's payment integration.
limitations: The free WhatsApp Business App supports basic catalog display (up to 500 products) but lacks the API-powered interactive catalog messaging, multi-product messages, and automated catalog sync.
Product catalog sync requires a BSP -- there is no native Meta plugin for WooCommerce. You depend on third-party BSP plugins to keep your catalog updated.

Catalog items are limited to what Meta's Commerce Manager supports: product name, description, price, image, URL, and retailer ID. Complex product variations (sizes, colors) require separate catalog entries or creative workarounds.
In-chat payments are not natively supported in most markets. Customers typically need to click through to your WooCommerce checkout page to complete payment.
Catalog updates can have sync delays depending on the BSP -- real-time inventory accuracy is not guaranteed across all providers.
Product catalogs must comply with Meta's Commerce Policy, which restricts certain product categories (alcohol, supplements, weapons, etc.).
pricing: Product catalog functionality is included with WhatsApp Business API access at no additional feature cost from Meta. However, sending catalog messages costs the standard per-message template rate (Marketing or Utility, depending on context). BSP platform fees vary: Interakt starts around $15/month, Wati around $39/month, Hello24 has a free tier for small catalogs. WooCommerce sync plugins from BSPs are typically included in their platform subscription. The real cost driver is message volume -- each product recommendation or catalog share sent as a template message incurs Meta's per-message fee.
scalability_ceiling: The WhatsApp catalog itself supports up to 500 products (same as the Business App). For WooCommerce stores with larger inventories, you will need to curate which products appear in your WhatsApp catalog. Multi-product messages max out at 30 items per message. Messaging tier limits (250/day to 100K+/day) apply to catalog messages just like any other template message.
setup_complexity: 3/5. For a WooCommerce store owner: (1) Set up WhatsApp Business API access through a BSP, (2) Connect your WooCommerce store using the BSP's WordPress plugin, (3) Map your product categories and sync your catalog, (4) Create catalog message templates and submit for Meta approval, (5) Configure automated product recommendation flows (optional). Most BSPs provide guided setup wizards. The WooCommerce plugin installation is straightforward (install plugin, enter API key, map fields), but the initial catalog sync and template approval adds 2-5 days to get fully operational.
migration_complexity: If moving from the free WhatsApp Business App to the API: your existing Business App catalog does not automatically migrate. You need to recreate or sync your catalog through the API/BSP. The upside is that if you are syncing from WooCommerce, your product data source stays the same -- you are just changing the connection method. Number porting from App to API causes the usual brief downtime and chat history loss.
data_compliance: Product catalog data (names, descriptions, prices, images) is stored in Meta's Commerce Manager. Customer browsing behavior within catalogs is processed by Meta. For GDPR compliance, ensure your BSP has a Data Processing Agreement covering catalog interaction data. Customer purchase data flowing back to WooCommerce should be handled according to your existing WooCommerce privacy policy.
verification_path: Business verification through Meta Business Manager is required for API catalog access. The green tick (OBA) is not required for catalog functionality but adds trust when customers browse your products in-chat. Meta Verified for Business is available for a monthly fee and can increase customer confidence in your catalog.
message_template_rules: Catalog messages sent outside the 24-hour service window require approved templates. Product recommendation messages are typically classified as Marketing (higher cost). Order confirmation messages that include product details can be classified as Utility (lower cost) if they do not contain promotional language. Single-product and multi-product message formats have specific template requirements that must be approved by Meta.
messaging_limits: Standard tier limits apply. Each catalog message counts as one message toward your daily limit. Sending bulk product recommendations to your contact list counts against your Marketing message limits. The ~2 marketing messages per user per day frequency cap applies to promotional catalog messages.
ai_automation_policy: AI-powered product recommendations are allowed as long as they use structured workflows (e.g., recommending products based on purchase history or browsing behavior using pre-approved templates). General-purpose AI chatbots that converse freely about products are banned under the January 15, 2026 policy. A structured product recommendation bot that walks customers through a decision tree to find the right product is compliant.
quality_risk: Sending too many unsolicited product catalog messages can trigger high block rates, pushing your quality rating to Yellow or Red. Best practice is to send catalog messages in response to customer interest signals (clicked a CTWA ad, visited your product page, asked about a product) rather than blasting your full contact list with promotional catalogs.
ads_integration: Click-to-WhatsApp Ads pair naturally with product catalogs. A customer clicks a CTWA ad for a specific product, lands in a WhatsApp conversation, and you can immediately share your product catalog or a targeted multi-product message. The 72-hour free messaging window means these catalog messages cost nothing in message fees during the CTWA window. CAPI conversion tracking can attribute purchases back to the original ad.
ecommerce_features: This is the core ecommerce feature of the WhatsApp Business API. Key capabilities: (1) Product catalog browsing in-chat, (2) Multi-product and single-product rich messages, (3) In-chat cart building, (4) WooCommerce catalog sync through BSPs like Interakt, Hello24, Wati, Spur, and Tellephant, (5) Automated order confirmation and shipping update messages tied to WooCommerce order status changes, (6) Abandoned cart recovery messages via Marketing templates. Shopify integration is more mature than WooCommerce at most BSPs, but WooCommerce support has improved significantly in 2025-2026.
wpchat_relevance: WPChat.com operates at a different layer than catalog functionality -- it handles the initial chat widget experience on your WordPress site. Where WPChat adds value in an ecommerce context: a visitor browsing your WooCommerce store clicks the WPChat widget, asks about a product, and the conversation can then be handed off to your WhatsApp Business API setup where catalog messages, product cards, and ordering flows take over. WPChat's chat funnels can pre-qualify purchase intent on-site before routing to your API-powered catalog experience. Without WPChat, you rely entirely on customers initiating contact through WhatsApp directly or via CTWA ads.
best_for: WooCommerce store owners who want to add WhatsApp as a sales channel with browsable product catalogs.
Ecommerce businesses running Click-to-WhatsApp Ads that want to show product catalogs immediately after ad click.
Stores with a curated product range (under 500 SKUs) that translates well to in-chat browsing.
Businesses where conversational commerce fits the customer journey -- high-consideration products, personalized recommendations, or markets where WhatsApp is a primary communication channel.
not_suited_for: Stores with massive catalogs (thousands of SKUs) where the 500-product WhatsApp catalog limit is too restrictive. Also not ideal for businesses selling restricted product categories under Meta's Commerce Policy. If your customers prefer a traditional web checkout experience and do not use WhatsApp for shopping, the ROI on catalog setup may not justify the effort.
cta_angle: For WordPress store owners, WPChat.com is the natural starting point: it puts a WhatsApp chat button on your site that captures purchase-intent conversations. From there, connecting a BSP for catalog sync turns those conversations into in-chat shopping experiences.
